title: senna foetidissima var. grandiflora
intro: Senna foetidissima var. grandiflora, also known as the var. grandiflora of the senna plant, is a type of shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a member of the Fabaceae family and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative due to its high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Senna foetidissima var. grandiflora include its ability to relieve constipation, reduce inflammation, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the leaves and pods, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or infusions, although caution should be taken when using it due to its potent and potentially side-effecting nature.
